title: Ultrasonic Levitation Demo
description: Acoustic levitation demonstration device using a 40 kHz ultrasonic transducer array and a reflector plate. Two Arduino-driven transducer arrays (transmit + reflect geometry) create a standing-wave pressure field that can levitate small particles (polystyrene, water droplets). A rotary encoder tunes the frequency; an OLED displays frequency and amplitude. Demonstrates acoustic radiation pressure principles.
objective: Generate a 40 kHz standing wave between transducer array and reflector (25 mm gap); levitate polystyrene beads 1–3 mm diameter; frequency tunable ±2 kHz around resonance; display frequency on OLED.
constraints: 5 V Arduino logic driving L298N half-bridge at 12 V for transducer array; 40 kHz piezo transducers; ≤ £30 BOM; 3D-printed frame.
supporting_fact_titles: Ultrasonic Proximity Sensor — ToF Distance Measurement
DC Motor Speed Control via PWM
Rotary Encoder — Incremental A/B Quadrature Pulse Counting
OLED SSD1306 Monochrome Display I²C Interface

element_usage_notes.Rotary Encoder User-Interface Navigation: KY-040; CLK D2 INT0; 100 Hz per detent (frequency sweep ±2 kHz); press to toggle output on/off.
element_usage_notes.SSD1306 OLED Display UI Module: 128×64; large frequency display (kHz) with 2-decimal resolution; output ON/OFF status; note on resonance peak (set by experimenter).
element_usage_notes.Arduino Uno/Nano MCU Platform: Uno R3; Timer1 generates a 50 % duty-cycle square wave on OC1A (D9) at the selected frequency. Timer1 ICR1 register set to drive frequency: ICR1 = (F_CPU / (2 × prescaler × freq)) − 1. L298N IN1/IN2 driven differentially (push-pull) from OC1A and its complement (OC1B). 12 V rail drives transducer array.

element_interactions.data: direction delta (±100 Hz per detent) + toggle press via GPIO INT
element_interactions.description: CW rotation increments frequency by 100 Hz; CCW decrements. Range 37–43 kHz. Press toggles Timer1 output (OC1A/OC1B enabled/disabled). Frequency stored to EEPROM for persistence.
Displays frequency with 0.1 kHz resolution. When output is ON, pulsing indicator shown. User observes levitation and notes resonance frequency visually.
